description_long: This object is a vintage-style electrical meter housed in a robust, rectangular metal enclosure. The front features a glass-covered display window with a visible analog dial and numerical readout, framed by a slightly raised metallic border. The enclosure is painted a dark, matte color and includes mounting brackets at the top and bottom, suggesting it is designed for secure attachment to a wall or panel. The sides are plain, with visible hinge and latch mechanisms indicating the front panel can be opened for maintenance or reading. The back is flat with additional mounting points and a serial marking, while the bottom has two protruding conduit entry points for electrical wiring. The overall construction appears durable, suitable for outdoor or utility environments.
description: A vintage, wall-mounted electrical meter in a dark metal box with a glass display window and mounting brackets. It features conduit entries and a hinged, latchable front panel.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glass-covered analog display and the metallic frame, with the main latch and mounting bracket visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the enclosure and the hinge mechanism for the front panel.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the flat back, mounting points, serial marking, and two conduit entry ports at the bottom.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the latch mechanism and the solid, unadorned side panel.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
